subject: Physics
title: Where Did All the Antimatter Go?
description: Learn Where Did All the Antimatter Go? in one minute.

hook.text: The Big Bang should have made equal amounts of matter and antimatter. They should have annihilated, leaving nothing.

buildup.text: Matter and antimatter destroy each other on contact, converting to pure energy.

discovery.text: Somehow, for every billion antimatter particles, there were a billion and one matter particles. That tiny excess is us.

twist.text: Nobody knows why this asymmetry exists. It's called baryogenesis and it's one of physics' biggest mysteries.

climax.text: If the balance had been perfect, the universe would contain only light. No atoms, no stars, no life.

punchline.text: One extra particle per billion. That tiny imbalance created everything.

quiz.question: Why does the universe contain mostly matter instead of antimatter?
options: A tiny unexplained asymmetry left slightly more matter than antimatter
Antimatter was pushed to the edge of the universe
